# 5 Trends Redefining Fraud

In the minute it takes you to read this paragraph, nearly 4,000 people will fall victim to fraud.That’s about 60 passwords, email addresses,…

[![Sift Author Logo](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20504%20504'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://sift.com/blog/author/sift-trust-and-safety-team/)

[Sift Trust and Safety Team](https://sift.com/blog/author/sift-trust-and-safety-team/)

Jul 9, 2018

![black-dot](https://sift.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/black-dot.svg)

![Press-Release-Tile-Image-Color-Pills\_Blue](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20683'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

In the minute it takes you to read this paragraph, nearly 4,000 people will fall victim to fraud.That’s about 60 passwords, email addresses, credit card numbers, or security questions stolen each second. When fraud happens that fast, anyone would find it difficult to keep up — even the sharpest fraud analyst or most innovative project manager.

That’s where this ebook comes in. We’ve done your homework, pored over the data, and interviewed experts and (former) fraudsters. Our goal? To narrow down five themes currently underpinning the world of fraud.

Armed with insights about these 5 trends, you can build a strategy that helps you delight and retain customers while growing your business. Whether you’re planning for the next month, quarter, or year, this ebook can be your guide.
